hct.source: Similar to hemoglobin, a low hematocrit can indicate anemia associated with RA.

hgb.source: Anemia is frequently observed in RA, particularly anemia of chronic disease, which can be detected by a low hemoglobin level.

mch.source: Low mean corpuscular hemoglobin (MCH) can also be associated with microcytic anemia in RA.

mchc.source: Low mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration (MCHC) can be seen in microcytic anemia in RA.

mcv.source: Microcytosis (low MCV) can be seen in RA, particularly in the context of anemia of chronic disease.

plt.source: Thrombocytosis is sometimes observed in RA, potentially due to inflammation or other factors.

rbc.source: Low red blood cell count can indicate anemia associated with RA.

rdw.source: Elevated RDW is a common finding in RA, reflecting increased red blood cell variation in size, often due to chronic inflammation.

wbc.source: Elevated white blood cell count can be seen in RA, reflecting inflammation and immune activation.
